text_fr: sa politique d'apartheid et d'autres politiques contraires aux  buts  et  aux  principes  du  Mandat,  et  que  toute tentative  de  protestation  contre  ces  r.:?litiques  ou  de resistance  a  ete  repri~ee  par  le  congediement,  l'arrestation,  la  deportation  et  l'exil des  personnes  interessees ainsi que de  dirigeants et de membres  des organisations politiques  africaines,
text_en: its policy of apartheid and other policies contrary to the purposes and principles of the Mandate, and that any attempt to protest against these r.?litics or resistance was reproved by the congedience, arrest, deportation and exile of interested persons and of leaders and members of African political organizations,

text_fr: Notant  avec  la  plus  vi'Ve  inquietude  et  un  profond regret que  les  forces  militaires  sud-africaines  stationnees  dans  le  Territoire  ont  ete  considerablement  renforcees  et que  la  police  locale,  avec  l'aide de  ces  forces, a perquisitionne  dans  les  maisons,  les  quartiers  et  l~s reserves  indigenes  pour  rechercher  des  pre~ves  d'ac1?vite  politique  et  expulser  des  zones  urbames,  considerees  comme  europeennes,  les  indigenes  non  porteurs de  laissez-passer,
text_en: Noting with the greatest concern and deep regret that the South African military forces stationed in the Territory have been considerably strengthened and that the local police, with the help of these forces, have searched homes, neighbourhoods and reserves indigenous to search for political opportunities and expel urban areas, considered to be Europeans, the indigenous who are not laissez-passer,

text_fr: N otant en particulier que  tous  ces  actes  sont  contraires  a la  lettre  et  a !'esprit du  Mandat  et  ont provoque  une  tension  et  une  agitation  croissantes  dans  le Territoire,
text_en: Noting in particular that all these acts are contrary to the letter and spirit of the Mandate and have caused increasing tension and agitation in the Territory,

items_fr: 4. Ex prime  tout  particulierement  sa  gratitude au President du  Comite,  M.  Enrique Rodriguez  Fabregat, representant de !'Uruguay, ainsi qu'aux Etats Membres qui  ont fait  partie du Comite,  pour le devouement avec lequel  iils  ont  exerce  leurs  fonctions.
items_en: 4. In particular, his gratitude to the Chairman of the Committee, Mr. Enrique Rodriguez Fabregat, representing Uruguay, as well as to the Member States which have been members of the Committee, for the dedication with which they have carried out their duties.

text_fr: Constatant  avec  la  plus  profonde  deception  et  un vif regret que  la  politique  et les  methodes inftexibles appliquees  par  le  Gou~e~nement  s~d-africain  dans \_ l'~inistration  du  Ternt01re,  contra1rement  aux obligations solennelles  qui  lui  incombent  en  ve~tu  . du  Mandat, oppriment  les  autochtones  et,  en  parttcuher,  que  qua› torze  Africains  ont  ete  inculpes  de  pretendus  actes de violence a la suite  des  troubles  survenus  en  decembre 1959  dans  le  quartier de  Windhoek,  au  cours  desquels onze  Africains  ont ete  tues  et  d'autres blesses  lorsque la police  et  des  soldats  ont ouvert  le  feu  sur une foule d'habitants  du  quartier  qui  protestaient  contre  leur transfert imminent  dans le nouveau quartier de Katutura,
text_en: Noting with deep regret that the policy and inflaming methods applied by the African Government in the initiation of the Terntre, in contrast to its solemn obligations under the Mandate, oppress the indigenous people and, in part, that four Africans were charged with pretensions of violence following the unrest in the Windhoek neighbourhood in December 1959, during which eleven Africans were killed and other injuries when the police and soldiers opened fire on a crowd of locals protesting against their imminent transfer to the new Katutura neighbourhood,

text_fr: N otant toutefois  que,  d'apres la  declaration  faite  par le Ministre  des  affaires  etrangeres  d'Afrique du  Sud a la  1218 9 seance  de  la  Quatrieme  Commission,  le 21  novembre  1961,  le  tribunal  a  estime  que  les  preuves dont  il  disposait  ne  justifiaient  pas  une  condamnation et  les  inculpes  ont  ete  acquittes,
text_en: Notwithstanding, however, that, according to the statement made by the Minister for Foreign Affairs of South Africa at the 1218 9th meeting of the Fourth Commission on 21 November 1961, the Court found that the evidence before it did not justify a conviction and the indictments were acquitted,

items_fr: l. Demande  tres  instamment au  Gouvernement  de la Republique  sud-africaine  et a I' Administration  du Sud-Quest africain  de  renoncer  immediatement a tous autres  actes  de  force  dans  le  Territoire  sous  mandat, destines  soit a reprimer les  mouvements  politiques afri› cains,  soit a appliquer des mesures d'apartheid imposees par la loi  et  les  reglements  administratifs,  de  s'abstenir de  poursuites  vexatoires  contre  les  Africains  pour des raisons  de  caractere  politique,  et  d'assurer  le  libre exercice des  droits  politiques  et  de  la  liberte  d'expres› sion a toutes  les  categories  de  la  population ;

2. Appelle  l'attention des  petitionnaires  interesses sur le  rapport du  Comite  du  Sud-Ouest africain relatif a la  situation  du  Territoire 21 et  sur  le  rapport  special du  Comite  relatif a la  mise  en  ceuvre  des  resolutions 1568 (XV) et  1596  (XV) de  l'Assemblee ienerale, en date  des  18 decembre  1960 et 7 avril  1961 , presentes a 1' Assemblee  lors de  sa  seizieme  session,  ains1  que  sur la  suite  que  l' Assemblee  a  donnee a ces  rapports.
items_en: l. Urges the Government of the Republic of South Africa and the Administration of South Africa to immediately renounce all other acts of force in the Territory under mandate, intended either to repress the African political movements, or to implement apartheid measures imposed by law and administrative regulations, to refrain from vexatious prosecution of Africans for reasons of political character, and to ensure the free exercise of political rights and freedom of expression to all categories of the population;

2. Draws the attention of the Petitioners Interessions to the report of the South West African Committee on the Situation of the Territory 21 and to the Special Report of the Committee on the Implementation of Resolutions 1568 (XV) and 1596 (XV) of the Ienerale Assembly of 18 December 1960 and 7 April 1961, submitted to the Assembly at its sixteenth session, and to the follow-up given by the Assembly to those reports.

text_fr: 1083·  seance  pleniere,
text_en: 1083th PLENARY SESSION,

text_fr: 19  decembre  1961.
text_en: 19 December 1961.

text_fr: 1704  (XVI),  Comite  du  Sud-Oueat  africain
text_en: 1704 (XVI), South West African Committee

text_fr: L' Assemblee generate,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Rappelant que,  par sa resolution  749  A  (VIII) du 28 novembre  1953,  elle  a  cree  le  Comite  du  Sud-Ouest africain,
text_en: Recalling that, by its resolution 749 A (VIII) of 28 November 1953, it established the South-West African Committee,

text_fr: 1083·  seance  pleniw,,
text_en: 1083. pleniw,

text_fr: 19 decembre  1961.
text_en: 19 December 1961.

text_fr: 1705  (XVI).  Programmee  epkiaux  d'enaeipe› ment et de  formation  pour  le  Sud.Oueat africain
text_en: 1705 (XVI). South Africa Training and Training Programme

text_fr: L' Assemblee generale,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: C onsiderant que  l'une  des  caracteristiques impor› tantes  de  la  politique  suivie  par  la  Republique  sud› africaine dans l'administration  du Territoire  sous mandat du  Sud-Ouest africain est de ne faire  beneficier les  habitants  autochtones  que  d'un systeme  rudimen› taire  d'instruction et  de  formation,  destine a confiner la  population  dans  des  occupations  serviles  afi.n  de la mamtenir  dans  un etat d'assujettissement a la minorite europeenne,
text_en: In view of the fact that one of the important features of the policy followed by the South African Republic in the administration of the Territory under the mandate of South-West Africa is to ensure that indigenous people benefit only from a system of education and training, it is intended to confine the population to servile occupations which are necessary to keep it in a state of subjection to European minorities,

text_fr: Considerant notamment  que  le  Gouvernement  sud› africain  prive  les  autochtones  du  Sud-Ouest africain de  la  possibilite  de  faire  des  etudes  secondaires  com› pletes  et des  etudes  superieures, au  Sud-Ouest africain ou  en  Afrique du  Sud,  et leur refuse  en  outre les  titres de  voyage  et  autres  moyens  qui  leur  permettraient de profiter  des  possibilites  d'etudes s'offrant a eux  dans d'autres regions,
text_en: Recognizing, inter alia, that the South African Government is depriving the indigenous people of South-West Africa of the opportunity to undertake comprehensive secondary studies and studies, in South-West Africa or South Africa, and further denying them the travel documents and other means to take advantage of the opportunities for study available to them in other regions,

text_fr: C onsiderant que  l'un des  devoirs  sacres  de l'Organi› sation des  Nations U nies  est de  favoriser :
text_en: In view of the fact that one of the sacred duties of the Organization of the United Nations is to promote:

items_fr: a) Le relevement des  niveaux de  vie,  le  plein emploi et des  conditions  de  progres et  de  developpement  cfans l'ordre economique  et  social,

b) La  solution  des  problemes  internationaux  dans les  domaines  economique et social  et  dans  celui de la sante  publique  et  la  solution  d'autres 1,>roblemes  con› nexes,  ainsi  que  la  cooperation  internationale  dans  les domaines  de  la  culture  intellectuelle  et  de  l' education,
items_en: (a) The raising of standards of living, full employment and conditions of progress and development in the economic and social order,

(b) The solution of international problems in the economic, social and public health fields and the solution of other problems 1,>roblemes conxes, as well as international cooperation in the fields of intellectual culture and education,

text_fr: Considerant que  l'Organisation  des  Nations  Unies a cree,  pour  s'acquitter de la tache qui  lui  incombe  en vertu de  l' Article  SS  de  la Charte,  des  dispositifs  d'as› sistance  economique,  social  et techniq\_ue et qu'une aide appreciable  a  ete  fournie  aux populations des  pays peu developpes,  notamment a celles  des  territoires  sous tutelle  et  des  territoires  coloniaux,
text_en: Recognizing the establishment by the United Nations of economic, social and technical support mechanisms for the fulfilment of its responsibilities under Article SS of the Charter, and that valuable assistance has been provided to the peoples of the less developed countries, including those of the Trust and Colonial Territories,

text_fr: Rappelant sa  resolution  1566  (XV)  du  18  decembre 1960,  par  laquelle  elle  a  invite  les  institutions  specia› lisees  et  le  Fonds  des  Nations  Unies  pour  l'enfance a aider au  developpement  du Sud-Ouest africain dans les domaines  economique  et social  et dans  celui  de  l'ensei-
text_en: Recalling its resolution 1566 (XV) of 18 December 1960, in which it invited the specialized agencies and the United Nations Children's Fund to assist in the development of South-West Africa in the economic, social and educational fields,

items_fr: Considerant que,  par  sa  resolution  1702  (XVI)  du 19  decembre  1961,  elle  a  cree  un  Comite  special  des Nations  Unies  pour  le  Sud-Ouest africain,
1. Decide de  dissoudre  le  Comite  du  Sud-Ouest africain;

2. Reconnait que  les rapports  presentes chaque annee  par le  Comite  et  les  rapports  speciaux  qui  lui ont  ete  demandes  ont  fourni a l'Assemblee generate d'utiles renseignements concernant la situation au SudOuest africain,  ce  qui  a  permis a l'Assemblee de s'appuyer  sur  ces  rapports  pour  exercer  ses  fonctions de controle  a l'egard du  Territoire  sous  mandat;

3. Remercie le  Comite  de  ses  constants  efforts  en faveur  de  la  population  du  Territoire  du  Sud-Ouest africain  et de  sa  contribution a l'accomplissement des taches de  }'Organisation des  Nations Unies;
items_en: Recognizing that, by its resolution 1702 (XVI) of 19 December 1961, it established a United Nations Special Committee for South-West Africa,
1. Decides to dissolve the South-West African Committee;

2. Acknowledges that the reports submitted by the Committee each year and the special reports that have been requested by the Committee have provided the General Assembly with useful information concerning the situation in South-West Africa, which has enabled the Assembly to rely on those reports to carry out its monitoring functions in respect of the Territory under mandate;

3. Expresses its appreciation to the Committee for its continued efforts in favour of the people of the South-West African Territory and its contribution to the fulfilment of the tasks of the United Nations;

text_fr: gnement, ainsi que  sa resolution  1527  (XV) du 15  de› cembre  1960,  relative  a  l'assistance aux anciens  terri› toires  sous  tutelle  et  aux  autres  nouveaux  Etats independants,
text_en: and its Resolution 1527 (XV) of 15 December 1960 on assistance to the former territories under guardianship and to the other new independent states,

text_fr: Reconnaisscmt en  particulier  le  besoin  urgent  d'as› surer a la population autochtone du  Sud-Quest africain, dans  le  domaine  de  finstruction,  des  progres  allant au-dela des limites imposees  par le  systeme  d'enseigne› ment bantou  applique  au  Sud-Quest  africain  et  en Afrique du Sud, et de  la  preparer a occuper des postcs dans  !'administration de  son  pays,
text_en: Recognise in particular the urgent need to ensure to the indigenous population of South Africa, in the field of education, progresses going beyond the limits imposed by the Bantu education system applied to South-West Africa and South Africa, and to prepare it to occupy posts in the administration of his country,

text_fr: Reconnaissant que  les  habitants autochtones  du Sud› Quest africain, dont le  pays peut etre considere a  juste titre  comme  economiquement  sous-developpe,  peuvent legitimement pretendre aux avantages  des  programmes de  cooperation technique des  Nations Unies,  mais n'ont pu,  jusqu'a present,  en raison  du  refus de la  Puissance mandataire  de preter  sa  cooperation  pour  qu'ils rei;oivent cette assistance, beneficier de ces  programmes,
text_en: Recognizing that the indigenous inhabitants of the South› African Quest, whose country can be rightly regarded as economically underdeveloped, can legitimately provide for the benefits of United Nations technical cooperation programmes, but have so far been unable to do so because of the proxy Power's refusal to provide its cooperation so that they may receive such assistance, benefit from such programmes,

text_fr: Reconnaissant  en  outre que  l'Qrganisation des  Na› tions  Unies  a  une  responsabilite  speciale  envers  les habitants  du  Territoire sous  mandat,
text_en: Recognizing further that the Qrganization of the United Nations has a special responsibility to the inhabitants of the Territory under mandate,

items_fr: 1. Fait  siennes Ies  recommandations  du  Comite  du Sud-Quest africain aux termes desquelles:

a) I1 faudrait  organiser  immediatement  un  pro› gramme special intensif de bourses pour former le plus grand  nombre  possible  d'autochtones du Territoire du Sud-Quest africain  aux fonctions  et  methodes  de  l'ad› ministration, ainsi  qu'a l'economie, au droit, a l'hygiene et  a  la  salubrite,  et  a  d'autres  disciplines  selon  les besoins;
b) I1  conviendrait en outre d'inviter les Etats Mem› bres a  offrir des bourses  a  des  etudiants du  Sud-Quest africain  pour  leur  permettre  de  faire  des  etudes  a l'etranger;

2. Decide d'instituer, a  !'intention de  la  population autochtone  du  Sud-Quest  africain,  un  tel  programme special de formation,  concernant notamment l'enseigne› ment technique, la formation  du  personnel de direction et la  formation  pedagogique;

3. Prie le  Secretaire general de tirer le  plus possible parti,  dans  la  mise  sur  pied  de  ce  programme  special de  formation  destine  a  la  population  autochtone  du Territoire,  des  programmes  de  cooperation  technique des  Nations  Unies qui  existent deja,  et notamment de permettre aux habitants  autochtones  du  Territoire qui resident  ou  se  trouvent  temporairement  resider  dans des pays ou territoires autres que ,le  Sud~Quest africain de  beneficier,  avec  le  consentement  et ,le  concours  des gouvernements  des  pays  d'accueil, desdits  programmes de cooperation technique  des  Nations Unies;

4. Invite les  institutions  specialisees  a  collaborer  a la mise  sur pied  et a  !'execution du  programme special de formation  susmentionne, en offrant toute !'assistance possible  ainsi  que  les  facilites  et  ressources  qu'elles peuvent fournir ;

5. Invite les Etats Membres a offrir aux autochtones du  Sud-Quest  africain,  directement  ou  par  l'interme› diaire  d'organisations benevoles,  des  bourses  d'etudes couvrant  tous  leurs  frais  tant  pour  l'achevement  de leurs  etudes  secondaires  que  pour  les  divers  genres d'etudes superieures;
6. Prie les  Etats  Membres  de  tenir  le  Secretaire general informe au sujet des bourses offertes et de celles qui  ont ete attribuees et utilisees ;
7. Pri, le  Secretaire  general  d'etablir un  dispositif approprie  permettant  l'examen des  demandes  presen-
items_en: 1. Endorses the recommendations of the South West African Committee that:

(a) A special intensive scholarship programme should be organized immediately to train as many indigenous people as possible from the South-South African Territory in the functions and methods of the ministry, as well as in the economy, law, hygiene and salubrite, and other disciplines as required;
(b) In addition, Member States should be invited to offer scholarships to students from South Africa to enable them to study abroad;
2. Decides to set up a special training programme for the indigenous population of South West Africa, including technical education, training of management staff and pedagogic training;

3. Requests the Secretary-General to make the fullest possible use of existing United Nations technical cooperation programmes in the implementation of this special training programme for the indigenous population of the Territory, including, inter alia, to enable the indigenous inhabitants of the Territory who reside or temporarily reside in countries or territories other than South Africa to benefit, with the consent and assistance of the Governments of the host countries, from such United Nations technical cooperation programmes;

4. Invites the specialized institutions to cooperate in the establishment and implementation of the special training programme referred to above, offering all possible assistance and the facilities and resources they can provide;

5. Invites Member States to offer scholarships to the indigenous people of South Africa, directly or through the intermediary of benevolent organizations, covering all their costs for the completion of their secondary studies as well as for the various types of higher studies;
6. Requests Member States to keep the Secretary-General informed of the scholarships offered and those awarded and used;
7. Requested the Secretary-General to establish an appropriate mechanism for the examination of pending applications

text_fr: tees  par  des  autochtones  du  Sud-Quest  africain  desi› reux de faire  des  etudes ou de recevoir  une formation hors du Territoire;
text_en: (a) the training of indigenous people from South Africa to undertake studies or to receive training outside the Territory;

items_fr: 8. Prie les  Etats  Membres  de  faciliter  les  deplace› ments  des  autochtones  du  Sud-Quest africain  desirant profiter  de  ces  moyens  d'enseignement;

9. Prie le  Secretaire general  de consulter le  Comite special des Nations Unies pour le Sud-Quest africain D en  ce  qui  concerne  l'application de  la  presente  reso› lution et  de  faire  rapport sur la question  a 1' Assemblee genera:le  lors  de  ses  sessions  ordinaires.
items_en: 8. Requests Member States to facilitate the mobility of indigenous people in South-West Africa wishing to benefit from these means of education;

9. Requests the Secretary-General to consult with the United Nations Special Committee on South Africa D on the implementation of this resolution and to report thereon to the General Assembly at its regular sessions.

text_fr: 1083·  seance  pleniere,
text_en: 1083th PLENARY SESSION,

text_fr: 19 decembre  1961.
text_en: 19 December 1961.

text_fr: 1743  (XVI).  Queetion  de l'avenir du Ruanda-Urundi
text_en: 1743 (XVI). Question of the future of the Ruanda-Urundi

text_fr: L' Assemblee generale,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Ayant  examine le  rapport  de  la  Commission des Nations  Unies  pour  le  Ruanda-Urundi 18 et ayant entendu  les  representants  du  Ruanda-Urundi et  de I' Autorite administrante ainsi  que  les petitionnaires,
text_en: Having considered the report of the United Nations Ruanda-Urundi Commission 18 and having heard the representatives of the Ruanda-Urundi and the Administering Authority and the Petitioners,

text_fr: Rappelant ses  resolutions  1579  (XV)  et 1580 (XV) du 20  decembre  1960  et  1605  (XV) du 21  avril 1961,
text_en: Recalling its resolutions 1579 (XV) and 1580 (XV) of 20 December 1960 and 1605 (XV) of 21 April 1961,

text_fr: Ayant presentes a l' esprit les  dispositions  de  la De› claration sur l'octroi de l'independance aux pays et aux peuples  coloniaux,  contenus  dans  sa  resolution 1514 (XV)  du  14  decembre  1960,
text_en: Bearing in mind the provisions of the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples contained in its resolution 1514 (XV) of 14 December 1960,

text_fr: Prenant en  consideration les  observations de la Com› mission  concernant  les  conditions  et ,l'atmosphere exis› tant au Rwanda et au Burundi avant les elections, ainsi que  l'organisation materielle  des  operations  electorales dans les  deux parties  du Territoire,
text_en: Taking into consideration the observations of the Commission on the conditions and the atmosphere in both Rwanda and Burundi prior to the elections, as well as the material organisation of the electoral operations in both parts of the Territory,

text_fr: Prenant note  ooec  satisfaction de  l'accord conclu,  le 8  fevrier  1962,  entre  le  Gouvernement  du  Rwanda et le  parti  d'opposition, l'Union nationale  rwandaise 24 ,
text_en: Noting with satisfaction the agreement reached on 8 February 1962 between the Government of Rwanda and the opposition party, the Rwandan National Union 24 ,

text_fr: C onsiderant que  le  retour  et  la  reinstallation  au Rwanda  des  milliers  de  refugies  n'a pas  ete  possible et  qu'un grand  nombre  d'entre eux  vivent  encore  en dehors  de  leur patrie,
text_en: In view of the fact that the return and resettlement of thousands of refugees in Rwanda has not been possible and that many of them still live outside their homeland,

text_fr: Reaf!irmant  sa  conviction que  le  meilleur  avenir  du Ruanda-Urundi  reside  dans  la  formation  d'un  Etat unique  constituant  une  entite  dans  les  domaines de l'economie, de  la  defense  et  des  relations  exterieures, sans  prejudice  de  l'autonomie interne  du  Rwanda  et du  Burundi,
text_en: Reaf!irming its conviction that the best future of Ruanda-Urundi lies in the formation of a single State constituting an entity in the fields of economics, defence and external relations, without prejudice to the internal autonomy of Rwanda and Burundi,

text_fr: Desireuse d'assurer le  plus  rapidement  possible l'ac› cession  a  l'independance du  Ruanda-Urundi  dans  les meilleures  conditions,
text_en: Desiring to ensure as quickly as possible the transfer to the independence of the Ruanda-Urundi in the best conditions,

items_fr: 1. Exprime sa  reconnaissance a. la  Commission  des Nations  Unies  pour  le  Ruanda-Urundi  et  a  la  Com› mission  speciale  d'amnistie,  creee  aux  termes de la resolution  1605  (XV)  de  l'Assemblee generale,  ainsi qu'a leur  personnel,  pour l'accomplissement des tiches dont  elles  ont  ete  chargees, et adresse  ses  felicitations aux populations du Ruanda-Urundi, aux dirigeants  des partis politiques,  aux representants de l' Autorite admi› nistrante,  a  tous  ceux  qui  ont  coopere efficacement avec  les  commissions;
2. Decide de creer une Commission pour le  Ruanda› U rundi  composee  de  cinq  commissaires  representant cinq  Etats  Membres,  elus par l'Assemblee generale,

11 Voir  r~solution  1702  (XVI)  du 19  dkembre  1961, ~r. 2. II Doewnntt.r of/iNl.r tU rAumtbll, 11hlral1, SIUWffll .r,.r,io,,, A,.,,,1,.r, additif  au  point  49  de  l'ordrc  du  jour (A/4994  et Add.1  et  Corr.1).
items_en: 1. Expresses its gratitude to the United Nations Commission for the Ruanda-Urundi and to the Commission for the Special Amnesty Mission established by Resolution 1605 (XV) of the General Assembly, as well as to their staff, for the fulfilment of the tasks entrusted to them, and extends its congratulations to the people of Ruanda-Urundi, to the leaders of the political parties, to the representatives of the Distinguished Authority, to all those who have cooperated effectively with the commissions;
2. Decides to establish a Commission for the Ruanda› U rundi composed of five Commissioners representing five Member States, elected by the General Assembly,
11 See Resolution 1702 (XVI) of 19 December 1961, r. 2. II Doewnntt.r of/iNl.r tU rAumtbll, 11hlral1, SIUWffll .r,r,io,, A,.,,1,r, addendum to item 49 of today's agenda (A/4994 and Add.1 and Corr.1).

text_fr: It Ibid., point  49  de  l'ordrc  du  jour,  document  A/C.4/532.
text_en: It Ibid., agenda item 49, document A/C.4/532.
